recommendations.title: Ensure NGINX only listens for network connections on authorized ports
recommendations.description: NGINX should be configured to listen only on authorized ports and protocols (TCP 80/443, UDP 443).
recommendations.rationale: Limiting listening ports ensures that no hidden or unintended services are exposed via NGINX.
recommendations.impact: Disabling unused ports reduces attack surface but might break HTTP/3 if UDP 443 is blocked.

recommendations.title: Ensure requests for unknown host names are rejected
recommendations.description: Catch-all default server blocks should reject unknown hostnames to prevent Host Header attacks.
recommendations.rationale: Prevents NGINX from serving applications to arbitrary domains pointing to the server's IP.
recommendations.impact: Direct access via IP or unconfigured CNAMEs will be rejected. This is intended behavior but requires that all valid domains are explicitly defined in their own server blocks.

remediations.note: After adding this block, ensure all your valid applications have their own server blocks with explicit server_name directives.
